description_long: This skyscraper is a tall, rectangular high-rise with a symmetrical, monolithic form and a muted beige color palette. The facade features vertical strips of windows set within recessed channels, creating a rhythmic pattern along the height of the building. The central section is flanked by two slightly protruding wings, each with a series of evenly spaced, dark-tinted windows. The roof is flat with a central cylindrical structure, possibly housing mechanical equipment or an elevator shaft. The exterior appears to be constructed primarily from concrete or stone, with glass used for the window sections. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on verticality.
description: A tall, beige skyscraper with vertical window strips and a flat roof, featuring a central cylindrical rooftop structure and minimal decorative elements.
description_view_0: Front view shows the central section with vertical window strips and two symmetrical wings, highlighting the building's monolithic and repetitive facade.
description_view_1: Side view is nearly identical to the front, emphasizing the uniformity and symmetry of the window pattern and facade.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, with the same vertical window arrangement and central cylindrical rooftop feature.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the consistent vertical window strips and symmetrical design, with no significant differences from other sides.
attribute_facade_design: The facade is composed of vertical strips of dark-tinted windows set within recessed beige concrete channels, creating a repetitive and monolithic appearance. The exterior is unadorned, with a focus on raw materiality and structural expression.
